draft.introduction: Amos delivers God’s devastating rejection of Israel’s worship: “I hate, I despise your feasts.” The demand for justice to “roll down like waters” became the defining statement of prophetic ethics.

translation.text: I hate, I despise your feasts, and I take no delight in your solemn assemblies. Even though you offer me your burnt offerings and grain offerings, I will not accept them. Take away from me the noise of your songs; to the melody of your harps I will not listen. But let justice roll down like waters, and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream.

translator_notes.note: The image of justice as a “rolling stream” (χείμαρρος) contrasts with the seasonal wadis of Palestine that dry up. True justice, like a perennial stream, never ceases. The prophetic tradition consistently subordinates ritual to ethics.
